2606.11555.title: End-to-End Machine Learning for Depressive State Classification via EEG and fNIRS
2606.11555.paper_summary: The escalating demand for mental healthcare, driven by rising societal stress, highlights the limitations of traditional psychiatric diagnostics. Conventional methods - relying primarily on clinical interviews and patient self-reports - are inherently vulnerable to subjective bias and the varying empirical judgment of practitioners. To address the need for quantitative evaluation, biological signal-based detection, including electroencephalography (EEG) and functional near-infrared spectroscopy (fNIRS), has emerged as a promising objective alternative. Such technology is particularly vital for identifying latent depressive states that may be unrecognized by the subjects themselves. Furthermore, in aging populations, the high comorbidity between depression and dementia necessitates early differentiation to prevent mutual symptom exacerbation and maintain Quality of Life (QoL). This pilot study of eleven healthy students establishes a framework for biological signal-based depression detection, serving as a foundational step toward automated, objective diagnostic tools for clinical use.
2606.11555.paper_summary_zh: 隨著社會壓力的上升，對心理健康護理的需求不斷增加，突顯了傳統精神病診斷的局限性。傳統方法主要依賴臨床訪談和病人自我報告，這些方法本質上容易受到主觀偏見和從業者的不同經驗判斷的影響。為了滿足對定量評估的需求，以生物信號為基礎的檢測技術，包括腦電圖（EEG）和功能性近紅外光譜（fNIRS），已成為一種有前景的客觀替代方案。這種技術對於識別可能未被受試者自身認識的潛在抑鬱狀態尤其重要。此外，在老年人群中，抑鬱症與癡呆症之間的高共病率需要及早區分，以防止相互症狀惡化並維持生活質量（QoL）。這項對十一名健康學生的初步研究建立了一個基於生物信號的抑鬱檢測框架，為臨床使用的自動化、客觀診斷工具奠定了基礎。
2606.11555.author: Riki Sakurai et.al.
2606.11555.authors: Riki Sakurai, Simon Kojima, Mihoko Otake-Matsuura, Shin'ichiro Kanoh, Tomasz M. Rutkowski
